content:
# ⚖️ Litigator / Practicing Attorney (US) | micro1

💼 *Company:* micro1  
📌 *Position:* Litigator / Practicing Attorney (US)  
📍 *Openings:* 30  
💰 *Pay:* *$100 - $150/hour*


### ✅ Key Responsibilities:
✔ *Legal reasoning*: audit AI on case law analysis, statutes, rules of civil procedure, and legal argument  
✔ *Litigation & practice expertise*: assess AI on pleadings, motions, discovery, depositions, and trial strategy  
✔ *Analytical rigor*: evaluate AI for issue spotting, application of law to facts, and logical consistency  
✔ +3 related: Legal writing, client counseling, negotiation, or regulatory analysis  
✔ Code/data quality: verify AI citations to case law, statutes, and FRCP. Check for hallucinations  
✔ Annotation: build datasets for legal Q&A, memo drafting, and litigation scenarios  
✔ Research: validate AI against primary sources, Westlaw/Lexis, and current law  
✔ Edge cases: flag AI giving incorrect law, unsafe legal advice, or jurisdictional errors  
✔ Deliver quality: ship 10-15 expert legal evaluations per day with 99%+ accuracy  

### ✅ Required Skills:
✔ *Legal reasoning*: JD from US law school + active US bar license required  
✔ *Litigation & practice expertise*: 3+ years practicing litigation. Federal and/or state court experience  
✔ *Analytical rigor*: Strong IRAC, issue spotting, and legal analysis skills  
✔ +3 related: Legal writing, motion practice, discovery, trial, or transactional work  
✔ Domain: Civil litigation preferred. Criminal, regulatory, or corporate also considered  
✔ Rigor: Catch AI errors in law, procedure, and factual application immediately  
✔ Communication: Clear legal writing and ability to explain complex law simply  
✔ Tools: Westlaw, Lexis, PACER, Bluebook citation  
✔ Bonus: Experience with LLM evaluation, legal tech, or teaching law  
✔ Bonus: Clerkship, BigLaw, or government practice experience  
✔ Must pass legal reasoning assessment + writing sample + bar verification + paid trial  

### ⚠️ Important Notes:
- This role involves evaluating AI outputs only. You will NOT be providing legal advice to clients.
- All work must comply with attorney ethics rules. AI outputs must include appropriate disclaimers.
- If you need legal advice: Consult a licensed attorney in your jurisdiction.

### 🎯 What you'll work on:
- Teaching AI to "think like a lawyer" - issue spotting and argument structure
- Reviewing AI drafts of motions, memos, and client communications  
- Building rubrics for legal accuracy and professional responsibility
- Improving AI understanding of procedure, evidence, and strategy

📢 *Apply and help set the standard for AI in law*
#Law #Litigation #Attorney #Legal #JD #AI #RemoteJobs #HiringNow

https://lnkd.in/dChHRPyq
comment: High bar for throughput + 99% accuracy makes source verification the real bottleneck—Westlaw/Lexis access latency and inter-annotator consistency will decide scalability more than legal skill.
